content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

August 30, 1976

Mr. William H. Moss
Post Office Box 266
Johannesburg, California 93528

Dear Mr. Moss:

     Thank you for your letter of August 12 regarding the
procedures by which the Conflict of Interest Code for the
Rand Communities County Water District was adopted.

     Government Code §87311 outlines the requisite procedures
for preparation of a Conflict of Interest Code. This Section
provides, in pertinent part, that the:

     ... review and preparation of Conflict of
     Interest Codes by local government agencies
     shall be carried out under procedures which
     guarantee to officers, employees, members,
     and consultants of the agency and to resi-
     dents of the jurisdiction adequate notice
     and a fair opportunity to present their
     views.

     Receipt of oral and written testimony provides interested
persons with a "fair opportunity" to express their views on
issues relating to a Code. Normally, written submissions are
made a part of the agency record with or without public dis-
cussion of the issues contained therein. While §87311, supra.,
does not require public discussion of written testimony, the
Commission's Regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18750(d)(3) requires,
with a limited exception, that all written submissions to an
agency regarding a proposed Code accompany the Code when sub-
mitted to the Commission for review. This provision is meant
to insure that any written testimony has received proper con-
sideration by the agency.

     As of this date, Rand's Code has not been received, and,
thus, we are unable to advise you on either the status of the
Code or the transmittal of the written testimony. A new dead-
line date of October 10 has been established for submission of
water district Codes, and this office should receive Rand's

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE/ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 · EXECUTIVE/LEGAL 322-5901 · ENFORCEMENT/CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6441

The image appears to be a mostly blank or empty page with very minimal visible text. There are only a few faint marks visible at the top of the page that are not clearly legible.

William H. Moss
August 30, 1976                                                    Page Two


Code on or before that date.  Your letter will be placed in
our file for this water district and will be reviewed by the
staff when the Code and the accompanying materials are re-
viewed.

     If you have further questions, please do not hesitate
to contact our office.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Sharon Stevens
                                        Conflicts of Interest
                                        Division

SS:gb

The image appears to be a mostly blank page with only a few small marks visible at the top (two circular marks that look like parentheses) and some minor specks scattered throughout. There is no substantial text content to transcribe from this document image.

P. O. Box 266
Johannesburg CA 935

August 12, 1976.

Fair Political Practices Commission,
P. O. Box 007,
Sacramento CA 95804.

Sirs:

On July 1, 1976, the board of directors of
Rand Communities County Water District, P. O. Box
198, Randsburg, California, 93555, following a public
hearing on that day, adopted a code of ethics on
conflict of interest and disclosure of personal
finances. The hearing was pursuant to public notice
which stated that at the hearing the board would
receive and consider oral and written testimony from
all interested persons. The code, so adopted, was the
to be forwarded to you for consideration together wit
all oral and written testimony so received.

Three separate items of written testimony
were handed to the board at the meeting. However, the
items were not considered by the board during the
course of the hearing.

I allege that the board acted illegally and
with bias in not considering the three items of writt
testimony and that the code of ethics so adopted was
therefore null and void.

I trust that you will let me know whether or
not the district's code of ethics forwarded to you wa
accompanied by no less than three items of written
testimony and what decision you have reached on the
matter.

Respectfully yours,

William H. Moss.

The image appears to be a mostly blank or very faint document page with minimal visible text. I can see what appears to be page markers or brackets at the top and bottom of the page, but the content is too faint or light to transcribe with accuracy. The page appears to be numbered "38" at the top left.

[The rest of the document content is not legible enough to transcribe accurately.]

Moss Post Office Box 266 Johannesburg, California 93528 Dear Mr. Moss: Thank you for your letter of August 12 regarding the procedures by which the Conflict of Interest Code for the Rand Communities County Water District was adopted. Government Code §87311 outlines the requisite procedures for preparation of a Conflict of Interest Code. This Section provides, in pertinent part, that the: ... review and preparation of Conflict of Interest Codes by local government agencies shall be carried out under procedures which guarantee to officers, employees, members, and consultants of the agency and to residents of the jurisdiction adequate notice and a fair opportunity to present their views. Receipt of oral and written testimony provides interested persons with a "fair opportunity" to express their views on issues relating to a Code. Normally, written submissions are made a part of the agency record with or without public discussion of the issues contained therein. While §87311, supra., does not require public discussion of written testimony, the Commission's Regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18750(d)(3) requires, with a limited exception, that all written submissions to an agency regarding a proposed Code accompany the Code when submitted to the Commission for review. This provision is meant to insure that any written testimony has received proper consideration by the agency. As of this date, Rand's Code has not been received, and, thus, we are unable to advise you on either the status of the Code or the transmittal of the written testimony. A new deadline date of October 10 has been established for submission of water district Codes, and this office should receive Rand's The image you provided is mostly blank, with very faint and mostly illegible text at the top.
